beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home while checking the weather and studio schedule.
beats.reasoning: Kenji wakes early as usual; the grey drizzle is already falling, so he checks his day plan and notes the SR-520 closure will complicate any Bellevue drive.

beats.activity: Drive south on I-5 to avoid SR-520 closure, heading to Bellevue for morning client consultation.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Kenji leaves early via I-5 to reach Bellevue before the closure window, adding buffer time for rain-slick roads.

beats.activity: Client consultation and design work at Bellevue studio space.
beats.reasoning: Morning slot with a new client discussing a large piece; perfectionist Kenji takes his time on the initial sketches and placement talk.

beats.activity: Grab coffee and pastry from a local café, wait out the worst of the rain before heading back.
beats.reasoning: Rain is steady until 2pm; Kenji stays inside, sips coffee, and avoids driving back across wet roads during peak closure window (9–noon has just passed).

beats.activity: Drive back north to Fremont studio via I-5 and local streets, rain easing slightly.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure is ending at noon; Kenji takes I-5 back north, a familiar route that avoids the bridge entirely and gets him home by late morning.

beats.activity: Tattoo session with an existing client at the Fremont studio.
beats.reasoning: Back at the studio for a solid afternoon slot; Kenji locks in and focuses on detail work—his perfectionist nature demands full concentration.

beats.activity: Lunch from a favorite food truck parked near Fremont; rain has mostly stopped by now.
beats.reasoning: Kenji knows the food truck rotation by heart; the rain clears by 2pm, so he steps out for a quick lunch at his favorite spot in the neighborhood.

beats.activity: Finish up paperwork and light admin at the studio; check tomorrow's schedule.
beats.reasoning: Kenji has the energy after lunch to handle studio logistics and prep for the next day; a slow Tuesday afternoon allows him breathing room.

beats.activity: Walk or bike to nearby climbing gym for an evening session to blow off steam.
beats.reasoning: After a day managing weather and road closures, Kenji needs physical outlet; climbing gym is in his neighborhood and keeps him grounded.

beats.activity: Climb, hydrate, chat with a close friend who's also there; low-key vibe.
beats.reasoning: Kenji prefers time with close friends over crowds; running into someone he knows makes the evening feel connected without being forced.

beats.activity: Walk to a nearby Japanese ramen spot for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Kenji's neighborhood has solid food options; a warm bowl of ramen after climbing feels perfect on a cool, damp evening.
(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Woke up to grey skies and checked the SR-520 closure right away—knew I'd have to take I-5 down to Bellevue for that morning consult. The drive was smooth enough, actually. Spent a couple hours working through design concepts with the client, then ducked into a café to wait out the heaviest rain with a pastry and espresso. By the time I headed back north, the drizzle was letting up. Got a solid tattoo session in at the studio—one of my regulars, clean work. Grabbed lunch from the food truck around Fremont, which hit different once the clouds broke. Finished admin stuff, then biked over to the climbing gym to shake off the day. My friend Maya was there, and we just climbed and talked about nothing important for a couple hours. Ended at Taneda for ramen, which was exactly what I needed. Felt good. Felt grounded.
thought_process: The bridge closure threw a wrench into my usual route, but I'd rather eat the extra drive time than deal with cross-lake gridlock. The morning consultation in Bellevue went well enough—decent deposit, solid design direction—so I wasn't stressed heading back. The rain made everything feel slower, quieter, which honestly suited me. I could've pushed more work in the afternoon, but I chose to stay light on it and prioritize the gym session instead. Days like this, when the weather's moody and the city feels a bit trapped, I need to move my body and be around people I actually like. Maya's presence was grounding. By the time I got to ramen, I felt like I'd metabolized the morning's logistics and actually lived the day, rather than just moving through it.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
